description: Loops that iterate over user-controlled arrays and perform external calls (transfers, approvals, or other contract calls) in each iteration can exhaust gas when the array is large. Each external call consumes gas; unbounded loops cause the transaction to hit the block gas limit and revert, resulting in denial of service. SCWE-109 covers unbounded loops generally; this weakness focuses on loops that perform external calls.
name: Gas Exhaustion via Unbounded Loops with External Calls
remediation: - Impose an upper bound on loop iterations or batch size. - Use pull-based patterns: let users claim individually instead of pushing to many addresses in one tx. - Process in chunks with pagination or checkpoints.
